examples.title: Ford-Fulkerson Algorithm
examples.description: Basic Ford-Fulkerson method for finding maximum flow
examples.code:
ALGORITHM Ford-Fulkerson(G, s, t)
BEGIN
  FOR each edge (u,v) in G
    f[u,v] ← 0
  WHILE there exists path p from s to t in residual graph Gf
    bottleneck ← MIN{cf(u,v) : (u,v) in p}
    FOR each edge (u,v) in p
      IF (u,v) is forward edge THEN
        f[u,v] ← f[u,v] + bottleneck
      ELSE
        f[v,u] ← f[v,u] - bottleneck
      ENDIF
    END FOR
  END WHILE
  RETURN f
END
examples.explanation: Repeatedly finds any augmenting path and increases flow by bottleneck capacity until no path exists.

examples.title: Edmonds-Karp Algorithm
examples.description: Ford-Fulkerson with BFS for shortest augmenting paths
examples.code:
ALGORITHM Edmonds-Karp(G, s, t)
BEGIN
  FOR each edge (u,v) in G
    f[u,v] ← 0
  WHILE TRUE
    queue ← EMPTY
    queue.enqueue(s)
    parent ← ARRAY[size(G.vertices)]
    WHILE queue not empty AND t not reached
      u ← queue.dequeue()
      FOR each v in adjacency[u]
        IF cf(u,v) > 0 AND v not visited
          parent[v] ← u
          queue.enqueue(v)
        ENDIF
      END FOR
    END WHILE
    IF t not reached THEN BREAK
    path ← reconstruct path using parent
    bottleneck ← MIN{cf(u,v) : (u,v) in path}
    augment flow along path by bottleneck
  END WHILE
  RETURN f
END
examples.explanation: Uses BFS to find shortest (fewest edges) augmenting path in each iteration, guaranteeing polynomial time.

examples.title: Find Minimum Cut
examples.description: Algorithm to find minimum cut after max flow computation
examples.code:
def find_min_cut(graph, capacity, flow, source):
    """Find minimum cut after max flow is computed"""
    n = len(graph)
    visited = [False] * n
    visited[source] = True
    queue = [source]
    
    # BFS to find reachable vertices in residual graph
    while queue:
        u = queue.pop(0)
        for v in graph[u]:
            # Check if edge has remaining capacity
            if not visited[v] and capacity[u][v] - flow[u][v] > 0:
                visited[v] = True
                queue.append(v)
    
    # S contains all visited vertices
    S = [i for i in range(n) if visited[i]
    T = [i for i in range(n) if not visited[i]]
    
    return S, T

# Calculate cut capacity
def cut_capacity(S, T, capacity, flow):
    total = 0
    for u in S:
        for v in T:
            total += capacity[u][v]
    return total
examples.explanation: After max flow, performs BFS in residual graph to find vertices reachable from source. Cut separates reachable from unreachable vertices.

examples.title: Bipartite Matching to Max Flow
examples.description: Transform bipartite matching to flow network and solve
examples.explanation: Converts bipartite matching to flow by adding source/sink with unit capacity edges, then runs max-flow algorithm.
